tasks.title: System Architecture & Architectural Decision Records
done_criteria: artifacts/architecture.json validates against schemas/architecture.schema.json

All four open questions resolved with documented ADR rationale: (1) vector storage technology (pgvector vs embedded vs managed service), (2) authentication model (API-key vs JWT/session for MVP), (3) latency SLA scope (whether 500ms p95 includes the LLM API call or only algorithmic scoring), (4) LLM provider/model selection
ADR files created for each decision: adr-001-vector-store.json, adr-002-auth-model.json, adr-003-latency-sla.json, adr-004-claude-model.json; each validates against schemas/adr.schema.json
Architecture confirms horizontal scalability path to 10,000+ developer profiles without architectural rework

Tech stack confirmed and documented: Python/FastAPI backend, React frontend, configurable LLM explanation provider (Phase-1 Google Gemini; Claude API supported), PostgreSQL relational store, chosen vector-compatible storage

Component diagram covers: profile ingestion service, matching engine, LLM explanation service, risk prediction engine, GDPR erasure handler, feedback/analytics service, vector embedding store, relational DB

tasks.title: REST API Contract Specification (OpenAPI 3.1)
done_criteria: artifacts/api-contracts.json is a valid OpenAPI 3.1 document

POST /api/v1/matches endpoint defined with request body accepting DeveloperProfile JSON + ProjectProfile JSON and response schema containing match_score (number 0.0–1.0), explanation (string), risks (array), growth_potential (array)
GET /api/v1/developers/{id}/risk endpoint defined returning burnout_risk_score (number) and bench_risk_score (number)
PUT /api/v1/config/weights endpoint defined with request body accepting weight map w1–w5 (floats summing to 1.0)
DELETE /api/v1/developers/{id} endpoint defined returning HTTP 204 on success; GET /api/v1/developers/{id} defined returning HTTP 404 when not found
POST /api/v1/matches/feedback endpoint defined with request body containing developer_id, match_id, and accepted (boolean)
GET /api/v1/analytics/rejection-rate endpoint defined with query parameter developer_id and response containing rejection_ratio
CRUD endpoints for DeveloperProfile and ProjectProfile ingestion defined
All response schemas, error codes, and authentication requirements documented per ADR-002 decision

tasks.title: Data Model & Storage Schema Design
done_criteria: artifacts/data-model.json defines DeveloperProfile entity with all required fields: id, skills, experience_years, preferred_stack, work_style vector, motivation_vector, timezone, availability_hours, career_goals, project_history

artifacts/data-model.json defines ProjectProfile entity with all required fields: id, required_skills, team_structure, workload_intensity, innovation_level, timezone_overlap_required, duration_weeks, growth_opportunities
MatchRecord entity defined capturing developer_id, project_id, match_score, explanation, risks, growth_potential, weights_snapshot, timestamp
FeedbackRecord entity defined capturing developer_id, match_id, accepted (boolean), timestamp
Vector embedding table/collection schema defined for behavioral and skill embeddings, consistent with ADR-001 chosen vector store
PromptVersion entity or artifact-reference schema defined to track versioned LLM prompt templates
AllocationRecord entity defined with project end_date and workload_intensity fields to support bench-risk and burnout-risk computation
GDPR erasure cascade documented for every entity: structured profile deletion, embedding purge, match history purge, feedback purge — all triggered by DELETE /api/v1/developers/{id}
Weight configuration storage defined (table or config file) supporting runtime update without code deployment

tasks.title: Backend Implementation (FastAPI, Matching Engine, Risk Engine, GDPR, Feedback, LLM Explanation Layer)
done_criteria: artifacts/code_spec.json validates against schemas/code_spec.schema.json and references artifacts/prompts/match_explanation_v1.json as the versioned prompt artifact

POST /api/v1/matches computes MATCH_SCORE = w1·skill_score + w2·workstyle_score + w3·motivation_score + w4·timezone_score + w5·growth_score with runtime-configurable weights and returns match_score, explanation (≥50 chars), risks (list), growth_potential (list) [AC1, AC2, AC6]
Behavioral dimension is demonstrably active: two developers with identical skills but opposing work_style vectors produce different match_scores confirming w2·workstyle_score is non-zero [AC2]

LLM integration calls the versioned prompt template (artifacts/prompts/match_explanation_v1.json); the prompt instructs the LLM to produce at minimum one skill-alignment statement, one behavioral/work-style statement, and one growth/career statement in every explanation [AC3]
No LLM prompt strings are inline in application source files; code_spec.json references the prompt artifact file path [AC12]
Changing artifacts/prompts/match_explanation_v1.json does not require modifying any .py source file [AC12]
PUT /api/v1/config/weights persists the new w1–w5 weight map; all subsequent POST /api/v1/matches calls on identical profile inputs return scores reflecting the updated weights [AC6]
GET /api/v1/developers/{id}/risk returns burnout_risk_score > 0.6 when developer’s allocation record shows ≥48 consecutive weeks at workload_intensity ≥ 0.8 [AC4]
GET /api/v1/developers/{id}/risk returns bench_risk_score > 0.7 when developer’s current project end_date is within 28 days and no follow-on allocation exists [AC5]

DELETE /api/v1/developers/{id} purges: the developer’s structured profile row, all associated vector embeddings, all MatchRecord rows, and all FeedbackRecord rows; returns HTTP 204; a subsequent GET /api/v1/developers/{id} returns HTTP 404 [AC9]
POST /api/v1/matches/feedback stores FeedbackRecord; GET /api/v1/analytics/rejection-rate?developer_id={id} returns the rejection ratio computed from stored feedback with ≥1 sample [AC10]
Manager-facing API endpoints do not return raw work_style vectors or motivation scalar arrays in any response body; only aggregated risk scores and badges are exposed [AC8]
Database migrations and seed data fixture (≥50 developer profiles, ≥20 project profiles) included to support local development and CI testing

tasks.title: Frontend Implementation (React Developer & Manager Dashboards)
done_criteria: Developer dashboard view renders ≥1 recommended project cards for any developer who has a complete profile with all required fields populated; each card displays match_score and LLM-generated explanation string [AC7]
Manager dashboard view displays per-developer risk badges (bench risk, burnout risk) and overall team composition health metrics [AC8]
Manager dashboard does not render raw behavioral vectors, motivation scalar values, or work_style array data in any UI component; only risk scores and badge states are displayed [AC8]
Both dashboards consume backend REST API endpoints as defined in artifacts/api-contracts.json
Frontend is implemented in React per tech-stack constraint
Frontend build completes without errors

tasks.title: Code Review
done_criteria: artifacts/review_report.json validates against schemas/review_report.schema.json
All 13 acceptance criteria reviewed and mapped to implemented code sections in the report
Prompt versioning verified: reviewer confirms no inline LLM prompt strings exist in any source file and that code_spec.json references the prompt artifact [AC12]
GDPR erasure cascade implementation verified: reviewer confirms DELETE triggers purge of profile, embeddings, match history, and feedback records [AC9]
Manager dashboard API responses verified to exclude raw behavioral vectors and motivation scalars [AC8]
Matching engine weight-configurability verified: weights are read at runtime from config/DB, not hardcoded [AC6]
Risk scoring logic reviewed for correctness against AC4 (burnout) and AC5 (bench) threshold criteria
No critical security vulnerabilities (e.g., SQL injection, unauthenticated deletion of other users’ data) identified without a documented mitigation
verdict field is one of: approved, approved_with_comments, rejected

tasks.title: Test Planning & Execution
done_criteria: artifacts/test_plan.json validates against schemas/test_plan.schema.json
Labeled 'good match' test scenario (high skill + work_style + motivation alignment) produces match_score ≥ 0.75; expected score range documented in test_plan.json [AC11]
Labeled 'bad match' test scenario (skill overlap but opposing behavioral vectors) produces match_score ≤ 0.45; expected score range documented in test_plan.json [AC11]

Behavioral dimension test: Pair A (identical skills, opposing work_style vectors) has strictly lower match_score than Pair B (identical skills, aligned work_style vectors), confirming w2 is non-zero and active [AC2]

Weight-change deterministic unit test: given known profile pair inputs, PUT /api/v1/config/weights with new weight map causes subsequent POST /api/v1/matches to return score delta within expected range [AC6]
Burnout-risk test: developer with ≥48 consecutive weeks at workload_intensity ≥ 0.8 returns burnout_risk_score > 0.6 [AC4]
Bench-risk test: developer whose current project end_date is within 28 days and has no follow-on allocation returns bench_risk_score > 0.7 [AC5]
POST /api/v1/matches response validation test: match_score is float 0.0–1.0, explanation is non-empty string ≥ 50 chars, risks is a list, growth_potential is a list [AC1]
Latency test: POST /api/v1/matches p95 latency ≤ 500ms verified under nominal load against local environment [AC1]
GDPR erasure test: DELETE /api/v1/developers/{id} returns 204; subsequent GET /api/v1/developers/{id} returns 404; confirmation that embeddings, match history, and feedback are absent [AC9]
Feedback and rejection-rate test: POST /api/v1/matches/feedback stores record; GET /api/v1/analytics/rejection-rate?developer_id={id} returns correct ratio with ≥1 sample [AC10]

LLM explanation structure regression test: explanation string contains at least one statement about skill alignment, one about work-style or behavioral alignment, and one about growth or career potential [AC3]
Automated integration test exercises the full match pipeline end-to-end: profile ingest → embed → score → LLM explain → response, runs against local or mocked environment and passes [AC13]
Developer dashboard test: dashboard renders ≥1 project card with match_score and explanation for a developer with complete profile [AC7]
Manager dashboard test: risk badges render; raw behavioral vectors and motivation scalars are absent from rendered output [AC8]
test_plan.json summary.failed == 0

tasks.title: Architecture & API Contract Update: Add Data Ingestion Service (in-place merge of architecture.json and api-contracts.json)
done_criteria: artifacts/architecture.json updated in-place to add a data-ingestion-service component listing five connector subcomponents: gitlab-connector, hr-connector, slack-connector, cv-connector, jira-connector [AC30]

Each connector subcomponent in architecture.json documents failure modes (rate-limit, missing-credentials, oversized-upload) with a degradation field describing the graceful fallback behavior for every failure mode [AC30]

artifacts/api-contracts.json updated in-place with OpenAPI 3.x path item definitions for: GET /api/v1/ingestion/connectors, POST /api/v1/ingestion/file, POST /api/v1/ingestion/gitlab, POST /api/v1/ingestion/jira [AC29]
ConnectorInfo schema component added to api-contracts.json components/schemas with at minimum kind ('file' or 'network') and availability ('live' or 'credential-gated') fields [AC29]

IngestionSummary schema component added to api-contracts.json components/schemas with fields: extracted (integer), enriched (integer), skipped (integer), created (integer), provenance ({llm: integer, heuristic: integer}), errors (array of strings), drafts (array) [AC29]
All /api/v1/ingestion/* path definitions in api-contracts.json specify HTTP 401 for unauthenticated and HTTP 403 for non-manager-role callers [AC29]
Updated artifacts/architecture.json and artifacts/api-contracts.json remain valid against their respective schemas after the merge

tasks.title: Backend: Connector Abstraction and Five Source Connectors (GitLab, HR, Slack, CV, Jira)
done_criteria: artifacts/code_spec/T-10-CONNECTORS.json validates against schemas/code_spec.schema.json

BaseConnector abstract class defined in src/connectors/base.py; connector yields SourceDocument records; SourceDocument dataclass/model carries: external_id, display_name, email, cv_text, git_log_text, slack_text, and optional timezone, availability_hours, experience_years, source fields

Every concrete connector exposes a kind attribute ('file' or 'network') and an availability attribute ('live' or 'credential-gated'); every connector degrades gracefully when credentials are absent, invalid, or the external service is unreachable — no connector raises HTTP 5xx or an unhandled exception

GitLab connector (src/connectors/gitlab_connector.py): implemented using httpx (not python-gitlab); pulls commit messages and merge-request titles into git_log_text; supports optional token and configurable base_url (default https://gitlab.com); respects per-request timeout and configurable GITLAB_MAX_PAGES page cap; missing or invalid token returns a degraded SourceDocument result, never an exception or HTTP 5xx [AC19]

HR connector (src/connectors/hr_connector.py): parses CSV or JSON file from in-memory bytes; case-insensitive and tolerant column mapping: name→display_name, email→email, title/role/bio→cv_text (skills appended), weekly_hours→availability_hours, years_experience→experience_years, timezone→timezone; yields one SourceDocument per employee row [AC17]

Slack connector (src/connectors/slack_connector.py): parses Slack export JSON from in-memory bytes; aggregates each user's channel messages into slack_text; yields one SourceDocument per user found in the export [AC18]

CV connector (src/connectors/cv_connector.py): parses a single .txt or .md file into cv_text yielding one SourceDocument; optional PDF support guarded by a conditional import (PyMuPDF or pdfminer); when PDF library is unavailable the connector reports itself as unavailable rather than raising an exception [AC15, AC16]

Jira connector (src/connectors/jira_connector.py): reads assigned issues, labels, and comments from Jira REST API via httpx; populates git_log_text; exposes interface documenting required credentials (base_url, email, token, project_key); missing credentials return a degraded IngestionSummary with errors list documenting what is required, never HTTP 5xx [AC20]
All connector files are new; no existing Phase-1 source files are modified by this task

tasks.title: Backend: Ingestion API Endpoints, ETL Orchestrator, Shared create-plus-embed Helper, and Settings (in-place merge of src/api/developers.py, src/core/settings.py, .env.example)
done_criteria: artifacts/code_spec/T-11-INGESTION-API.json validates against schemas/code_spec.schema.json

GET /api/v1/ingestion/connectors returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array containing ≥5 ConnectorInfo descriptors (gitlab, hr, slack, cv, jira); each descriptor includes kind ('file' or 'network') and availability ('live' or 'credential-gated'); endpoint secured by require_manager dependency from src/core/auth.py [AC14]

POST /api/v1/ingestion/file accepts multipart upload (file + source + mode); enforces MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES before any parsing — requests exceeding the limit receive HTTP 413; no data written to disk at any point [AC22]
POST /api/v1/ingestion/gitlab accepts JSON body (username, project, optional base_url, optional token, mode); delegates to GitLab connector [AC19]

POST /api/v1/ingestion/jira accepts JSON body (base_url, email, token, project_key, usernames, mode); delegates to Jira connector; missing or invalid credentials return HTTP 200 with degraded IngestionSummary (errors list), never HTTP 5xx [AC20]
All /api/v1/ingestion/* endpoints use require_manager dependency from src/core/auth.py; developer-role JWTs receive HTTP 403; unauthenticated requests receive HTTP 401 [AC21]

Shared create-plus-embed helper extracted into src/core/helpers.py (new file); src/api/developers.py updated in-place to import and call the shared helper; POST /api/v1/developers and commit-mode ingestion share a single code path with no duplication [AC16, AC23]

ETL orchestrator (src/etl/orchestrator.py) calls enrich_profile(cv_text, git_log_text, slack_text) for each SourceDocument via asyncio.to_thread; records where enrich_profile returns empty skills list are counted in IngestionSummary.skipped and no DeveloperProfile row is created; remaining records in the batch continue processing and HTTP response is 200 [AC23, AC24]

IngestionSummary provenance object populated with llm and heuristic integer counts derived from EnrichmentResult.provenance for each enriched record; provenance.llm + provenance.heuristic equals enriched count [AC25]
Preview mode (mode=preview): returns IngestionSummary with drafts for human review; IngestionSummary.created equals 0; no DeveloperProfile row persisted [AC15]

Commit mode (mode=commit): creates DeveloperProfile rows via the shared create-plus-embed helper; enqueues embeddings asynchronously via BackgroundTasks; IngestionSummary.created ≥1 for successful ingestion [AC16]
INGESTION_MAX_RECORDS batch cap (default 500) enforced before processing begins; batches exceeding the cap are truncated with a note in IngestionSummary.errors

src/core/settings.py updated in-place to add: GITLAB_BASE_URL (default https://gitlab.com), GITLAB_TOKEN (default empty string), JIRA_BASE_URL (default empty string), JIRA_EMAIL (default empty string), JIRA_TOKEN (default empty string), MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES (default 10485760), INGESTION_MAX_RECORDS (default 500), GITLAB_MAX_PAGES (default 10) [AC28]
.env.example updated in-place to document all eight new environment variables with safe defaults and inline comments [AC28]

tasks.title: Frontend: Ingestion Page and Multipart Client Helper (in-place merge of frontend/src/api/client.ts)
done_criteria: artifacts/code_spec/T-12-INGESTION-FRONTEND.json validates against schemas/code_spec.schema.json

IngestionPage (frontend/src/pages/IngestionPage.tsx) rendered exclusively for manager-role sessions; wired into existing tab pattern without react-router, matching the convention used by ManagerDashboard and other existing pages [AC26]

ConnectorPicker component (frontend/src/components/ConnectorPicker.tsx) populated from GET /api/v1/ingestion/connectors; credential-gated connectors (availability == 'credential-gated') rendered as disabled with a tooltip stating required credentials [AC26]
File dropzone within IngestionPage accepts CSV, JSON, TXT, MD files for file-kind connectors; file selection invokes POST /api/v1/ingestion/file with multipart body [AC26]

GitLab input form renders fields: username (required), project (optional), token (optional); Jira input form renders fields: base_url (required), email (required), token (required), project_key (required), usernames (required) [AC26]
Preview button posts selected source data in mode=preview and renders the returned IngestionSummary.drafts in a scrollable DraftReviewTable component [AC26]
Approve and Create button re-posts in mode=commit; shows updated IngestionSummary.created count to user upon completion [AC26]

All interactive elements (connector picker, file dropzone, GitLab fields, Jira fields, preview button, approve-and-create button) carry data-testid attributes following the existing codebase naming pattern [AC26]
Multipart-capable request helper (uploadFile or equivalent) added to frontend/src/api/client.ts via in-place merge; existing client functions are not altered [AC26]
Frontend build completes without errors after the merge

tasks.title: Code Review: Data Pipeline & Ingestion Increment
done_criteria: artifacts/review_report_ingestion.json validates against schemas/review_report.schema.json

Graceful degradation verified for all five connectors: missing/invalid GitLab token returns degraded result (never HTTP 5xx); Jira missing credentials returns HTTP 200 with errors list; HR/Slack/CV parse failures degrade per-record without aborting the batch [AC19, AC20]
Auth enforcement verified: require_manager from src/core/auth.py applied to every /api/v1/ingestion/* route; developer-role path returns HTTP 403; unauthenticated path returns HTTP 401 [AC21]
File upload memory safety verified: MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES guard applied before any parsing; no disk writes in any connector or endpoint; HTTP 413 returned for oversized payloads [AC22]

Shared create-plus-embed helper verified: src/core/helpers.py contains the shared function; both src/api/developers.py (POST /api/v1/developers) and src/etl/orchestrator.py (commit mode) import and call the same helper; no duplication [AC16, AC23]

ETL orchestrator reviewed: asyncio.to_thread wraps enrich_profile calls correctly; records with empty skills list are counted in IngestionSummary.skipped and skipped from DeveloperProfile creation; provenance.llm + provenance.heuristic equals enriched count [AC23, AC24, AC25]

Preview vs commit mode boundary verified: preview mode creates zero DeveloperProfile rows and returns drafts only; commit mode persists via shared helper and enqueues embeddings via BackgroundTasks [AC15, AC16]

Frontend Ingestion page reviewed: manager-role guard present; connector picker disabled for credential-gated entries with tooltip; all data-testid attributes present; multipart client helper added without breaking existing client.ts functions [AC26]
Constraint confirmed: no existing Phase-1 test file modified by T-10 or T-11; all new tests are fully mocked and offline; 120 existing tests must remain green
verdict field in artifacts/review_report_ingestion.json is one of: approved, approved_with_comments, rejected

GET /ingestion/connectors test: returns HTTP 200 with ≥5 ConnectorInfo entries each having kind and availability; HTTP 403 for developer-role JWT; HTTP 401 for unauthenticated request [AC14]
POST /ingestion/file CV preview test: source=cv, valid UTF-8 .txt body, mode=preview returns HTTP 200; IngestionSummary.created == 0; at least one draft entry in response [AC15]

POST /ingestion/file CV commit test: source=cv, valid UTF-8 .txt body, mode=commit returns HTTP 200; IngestionSummary.created ≥1; DeveloperProfile persisted via shared helper (verified through MockAsyncSession.added); embeddings enqueued via BackgroundTasks mock [AC16]

POST /ingestion/file HR CSV column-mapping test: source=hr, valid CSV payload returns one draft per employee row; case-insensitive column names title/role/bio→cv_text, weekly_hours→availability_hours, years_experience→experience_years handled correctly [AC17]
POST /ingestion/file Slack JSON test: source=slack, valid Slack export JSON returns one draft per user; user channel messages concatenated as slack_text [AC18]

POST /ingestion/gitlab mock-transport test: mock httpx transport returning commit and MR data → HTTP 200 with IngestionSummary.drafts[0].git_log_text containing mocked commit messages and MR titles; called without token (or with invalid token) → HTTP 200 with degraded (empty or partial) IngestionSummary, never HTTP 5xx [AC19]

POST /ingestion/jira missing-credentials test: request with missing or invalid credentials → HTTP 200 with IngestionSummary.errors containing a message documenting the missing credential; endpoint never returns HTTP 5xx regardless of credential state [AC20]
Auth boundary tests: all /api/v1/ingestion/* endpoints return HTTP 403 for developer-role JWT and HTTP 401 for unauthenticated request; manager-role JWT accepted for all operations [AC21]
HTTP 413 upload-size test: file body exceeding MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES returns HTTP 413 with structured error body; no DeveloperProfile created; no disk writes occurred [AC22]

enrich_profile skipped-record test: when enrich_profile returns empty skills list for a SourceDocument that record is counted in IngestionSummary.skipped; all other records in the same batch continue processing; HTTP response is still 200 [AC24]
IngestionSummary.provenance test: provenance.llm + provenance.heuristic equals IngestionSummary.enriched for a multi-record batch with mixed EnrichmentResult.provenance values [AC25]

End-to-end integration test (tests/integration/test_ingestion_pipeline.py): HR CSV connector extraction yields one SourceDocument per row → enrich_profile mocked to return deterministic EnrichmentResult → commit mode persists DeveloperProfile rows (verified via MockAsyncSession.added) → embeddings enqueued (verified via BackgroundTasks mock); all HTTP calls use mock httpx transport; no live database or external API connections required; test passes in CI alongside all 120 existing tests which remain green [AC27]

All new tests (test_connectors.py, test_ingestion_api.py, test_ingestion_auth.py, test_ingestion_pipeline.py) are fully mocked and offline; GitLab and Jira HTTP interactions use mock httpx transport; file connectors tested from in-memory bytes
